message:
fix(ft-conformance): update device-mismatch assertions and normalize formatting

Two categories of changes:

1. Error message alignment: the device-mismatch rejection test in both
   lib.rs (unit test) and tests/smoke.rs (integration test) asserted
   against the old error text ("incompatible dispatch keyset" /
   "AutogradCPU requires CPU backend availability"). With the dispatch
   layer now returning structured TensorCompatError::DeviceMismatch
   errors instead of generic keyset incompatibility messages, update
   assertions to match the new error surface: "device mismatch" and
   "lhs=Cuda, rhs=Cpu". This keeps conformance tests in sync with the
   dispatch hardening done in ft-dispatch and ft-api.

2. Formatting: normalize multi-line assert! macro calls across the test
   suite. Several assert!(expr, "message") calls had the condition and
   message on a single long line, exceeding the project's implicit line
   width. Break these into multi-line form for consistency with the rest
   of the conformance crate. Also collapse a few unnecessary multi-line
   closures in emit_e2e_forensics_matrix_filtered_legacy into single-
   line form where the closure body is trivially short.

No behavioral changes to conformance logic itself.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
